free_text: Excitation functions deuteron induced nuclear reactions
on natural zinc up to 50 MeV
CGR 980 cyclotron of the UCL, Belgium
CGR 560 cyclotron of the VUB, Belgium
Targets were high purity Zn foils of 21.9 microns.
Stacked target technique was used. Irradiation was done
in vacuum with deuteron beam at 50 and 30 MeV primary
beam energies and 40 nA for 30 minutes.
High resolution gamma spectrometry was applied without
chemical separation.
The stacks contained besides the target foils Al, Ni
and Cu foils as monitor and energy degrader foils.
Beam current was measured in Faraday-cup and also
monitored by nuclear reactions on Al, Ni and Cu.
Energy degradation was calculated by using the
polynomial approximation of Andersen and Ziegler.
Energy scale and beam intensity along the stacks were
corrected according to the applied monitor reactions.
The whole excitation functions of the monitor reactions
was remeasured and compared to the standard or
recommended values.
Decay data were taken from the on-line database of Lund
The whole excitation
function of the used monitor reactions were remeasured
and compared with the recommended curve.
chapter 4
The uncertainty of the primary beam 0.3 MeV.
The energy uncertainty varies along the stack. It was
+-0.5 MeV on the first foil and about 2.0 MeV on the
last foil of a long stack. Energy scale was corrected
by using the monitor foils.
Total errors were given. Correction of the beam
intensity was made using the monitor reactions.
Contributing errors:
number of target nuclei           5%
beam intensity                    3%
detector efficiency               5%
peak area and statistical error 1-5%
nuclear data                    1-5%
Ts
Ref.in MONIT-REF corrected.
